ux_issues.description: Usage endpoint reports 6 messages used but 7 were sent (1 selection + 6 planning). The selection-mode message may not count toward the daily limit, which is undocumented.
ux_issues.suggestion: Clarify in /api/usage whether selection-mode messages count toward the daily limit.

ux_issues.description: Some days have apparent duplicate items with similar titles (e.g., Day 9 has 'Huayna Picchu Hike & Return to Cusco' and 'Huayna Picchu Research Hike & Return to Cusco'; Day 10 has two similar transit items; Days 11-14 show overlapping items from different turns). This is because successive turns add items to existing days without deduplicating against existing items.
ux_issues.suggestion: Consider deduplication or at least warn the AI about existing items on target days to avoid near-duplicates.

usefulness_evaluation.narrative: Excellent fabrication detection performance. All 6 planning turns behaved correctly: Turns 1-5 all called create_itinerary_items as required, Turn 6 (summary) correctly did not call it. Zero fabricated tool successes detected. Item progression: Turn 1=19, Turn 2=32, Turn 3=37 (append, no overwrite — critical test passed), Turn 4=46, Turn 5=58, Turn 6=58 (unchanged). Final itinerary: 58 items across 14 days covering Cusco (days 1-4), Sacred Valley (days 5-7), Machu Picchu (day 8), transit/extra (days 9-10), Lake Titicaca (days 11-12), and return Lima (days 13-14). The AI proactively built a full 2-week itinerary starting from Turn 1. Minor issue: some near-duplicate items on later days from overlapping tool calls across turns. 3 fabrication-phrase matches total across all turns, all backed by actual tool calls (no false claims).
